LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT DAVID SHERMAN DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEMURRER OVERRULED REGARDING STANDING ISSUE. EXECUTRIX'S FINAL ACCOUNT AND REPORT DESCRIBES HER ACTIONS IN ITEM 7 ASSIGNING THE RIGHTS OF THE ESTATE TO PLAINTIFF. JUDGE APPROVED OF EXECUTRIX'S ACTIONS IN ORDER DATED SEPTEMBER 29, 2009. DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO 2ND, 3RD, 4TH, 5TH, 7TH AND 8TH CAUSES OF ACTION. INSUFFICIENT FACTS PLED THAT DEFENDANT INTENTIONALLY DISRUPTED OR BREACHED A RELATIONSHIP BETWEEN PLAINTIFF AND WELLS FARGO, OR THAT A RELATIONSHIP EXISTED. FRAUD MUST BE SUPPORTED BY SPECIFIC FACTS, AND CONSTRUCTIVE FRAUD MUST BE SHOWN BY MISLEADING CONDUCT OR AN INHERENTLY FRAUDULENT ACT OR OMISSION. FACTS PLED DO NOT SHOW DEFENDANT'S RETENTION OF THE INSURANCE PROCEEDS WAS UNJUST. PLAINTIFF DOES NOT ALLEGE FACTS SUFFICIENT TO SHOW HE HAD POSSESSION OF THE INSURANCE PROCEEDS OR THAT THE DISPOSITION WAS WRONGFUL. PLAINTIFF MUST ALLEGE WRONGFUL ACTS DEFENDANT PERFORMED TO OBTAIN THE LOAN AND RECEIVE THE INSURANCE PROCEEDS. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797
1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF BELISLE, NORMAN JAMES AS TO DEFENDANT SHERMAN, DAVID A. WELLS FARGO & COMPANY, INC. MINNESOTA LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY, INC. DOES 1-20, INCLUSIVE

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT DAVID SHERMAN DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO 3RD CAUSE OF ACTION FOR FRAUD AND 4TH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR CONSTRUCTIVE FRAUD. THE ALLEGED MISREPRESENTATION ON WHICH THEY ARE BASED DO NOT STATE A CLAIM WITH RESPECT TO PLAINTIFF. PLAINTIFF DOES NOT ALLEGE THAT A MISREPRESENTATION WAS MADE TO HIM OR THAT HE RELIED ON IT. DEMURRER OVERRULED AS TO ALL OTHER CAUSES OF ACTION. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD; COURT REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT DAVID A. SHERMAN MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT; OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. WHILE PLAINTIFF DOES HAVE STANDING TO MAINTAIN THIS ACTION UNDER CALIFORNIA LAW, EXCEPT IN SPECIFIED SITUATIONS (NOT PRESENT HERE), A WILL CANNOT OPERATE TO CHANGE THE BENEFICIARY DESIGNATED ON A LIFE INSURANCE POLICY. COOK V. COOK, (1941) 17 CAL.2ND 639, MOSS V. WARREN, (1975) 43 CAL.APP.3RD 651, 656. FURTHER, PLAINTIFF'S ARGUMENT OF SUBSTANTIAL COMPLIANCE FAILS. DECEDENT HAD 3 MONTHS BEFORE HIS DEATH TO CHANGE BENEFICIARIES. HE WAS ABLE TO CHANGE HIS WILL JUST PRIOR TO DEATH, YET TOOK NO ACTION TO CHANGE THE BENEFICIARY ON HIS POLICY. FINALLY, PLAINTIFF FAILS TO RAISE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT BASED ON THE HFC LOAN AS THERE IS NOT EVIDENCE TO SUPPORT THIS CONTENTION. PROPOSED ORDER SUBMITTED BY MOVING PARTY. IF PLAINTIFF DOES NOT AGREE WITH THE FORM OF ORDER, PLAINTIFF MAY SUBMIT ITS OWN FOR THE COURT TO DETERMINE. (IN ADDITION, THE COURT INDICATED THAT WITH MINOR EXCEPTIONS, THE OBJECTIONS TO EVIDENCE WERE OVERRULED.) JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI, REPORTER: KARLA ELLIS-DAVIS, CSR #12998

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS MINNESOTA LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY AND WELLS FARGO & COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, AND ALTERNATIVE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - DEFENDANTS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED FOR SAME REASONS SET FORTH IN CO-DEFENDANT'S MOTION. [WHILE PLAINTIFF DOES HAVE STANDING TO MAINTAIN THIS ACTION UNDER CALIFORNIA LAW, EXCEPT IN SPECIFIED SITUATIONS (NOT PRESENT HERE), A WILL CANNOT OPERATE TO CHANGE THE BENEFICIARY DESIGNATED ON A LIFE INSURANCE POLICY. COOK V. COOK, (1941) 17 CAL.2ND 639, MOSS V. WARREN, (1975) 43 CAL.APP.3RD 651, 656. FURTHER, PLAINTIFF'S ARGUMENT OF SUBSTANTIAL COMPLIANCE FAILS. DECEDENT HAD 3 MONTHS BEFORE HIS DEATH TO CHANGE BENEFICIARIES. HE WAS ABLE TO CHANGE HIS WILL JUST PRIOR TO DEATH, YET TOOK NO ACTION TO CHANGE THE BENEFICIARY ON HIS POLICY. FINALLY, PLAINTIFF FAILS TO RAISE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT BASED ON THE HFC LOAN AS THERE IS NOT EVIDENCE TO SUPPORT THIS CONTENTION.] PROPOSED ORDER SUBMITTED BY MOVING PARTY. IF PLAINTIFF DOES NOT AGREE WITH THE FORM OF ORDER, PLAINTIFF MAY SUBMIT ITS OWN FOR THE COURT TO DETERMINE. (IN ADDITION, THE COURT INDICATED THAT WITH MINOR EXCEPTIONS, THE OBJECTIONS TO EVIDENCE WERE OVERRULED.) JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI, REPORTER: KARLA ELLIS-DAVIS, CSR #12998

No appearance on behalf or by Cross-Defendant Norman James Belisle.
Cross-Defendant David Sherman sworn and examined on his own behalf.
Court Ordered Cross-Defendants Roes 1-20 dismissed without prejudice.
Exhibit 1. Certificate of Domestic Partnership Id/Evid.
Exhibit 2. Quitclaim Deed Id/Evid.
Exhibit 3. Several documents Wachovia Bank Mortgage Loan Id/Evid.

Exhibit 8. Compilation of billing statements from Law Firm Id/Evid.

After evidence, Court ordered the following Judgment entered in favor of Cross-Complainant David Sherman and against Cross-Defendant Norman James Belisle as follows:
Compensatory Damages $33,290.00, plus allowable costs of suit, subject to proof via filing of Memorandum of Costs.
Judgment signed in open court.
Court ordered exhibits returned to plaintiff's counsel for the care and custody of the court.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar May-20-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. PLAINTIFF NORMAN BELISLE'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS TO THE 1ST, 2ND, 3RD, 4TH, 5TH, 6TH, 7TH AND 8TH CAUSES OF ACTION AGAINST DEFENDANTS is off calendar per moving party. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar May-18-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANT DAVID A. SHERMAN MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT; OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION.

APPEARANCES: Drexel A. Bradshaw, Esq. & Alison B. Kwan, Esq. of Bradshaw & Associates, P.C. 433-4800 for pltf & opposing party; A. Jeanne Jorge-Grove, Esq. & Paul H. Melbostad, Esq. of Goldstein, Gellman, Melbostad & Harris LLP 673-5600 for deft & moving party David A. Sherman; Linda B. Oliver, Esq. of Reed Smith LLP 659-4819 for defts Minnesota Life Insurance Company and Wells Fargo & Company.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. WHILE PLAINTIFF DOES HAVE STANDING TO MAINTAIN THIS ACTION UNDER CALIFORNIA LAW, EXCEPT IN SPECIFIED SITUATIONS (NOT PRESENT HERE), A WILL CANNOT OPERATE TO CHANGE THE BENEFICIARY DESIGNATED ON A LIFE INSURANCE POLICY. COOK V. COOK, (1941) 17 CAL.2ND 639, MOSS V. WARREN, (1975) 43 CAL.APP.3RD 651, 656. FURTHER, PLAINTIFF'S ARGUMENT OF SUBSTANTIAL COMPLIANCE FAILS. DECEDENT HAD 3 MONTHS BEFORE HIS DEATH TO CHANGE BENEFICIARIES. HE WAS ABLE TO CHANGE HIS WILL JUST PRIOR TO DEATH, YET TOOK NO ACTION TO CHANGE THE BENEFICIARY ON HIS POLICY. FINALLY, PLAINTIFF FAILS TO RAISE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT BASED ON THE HFC LOAN AS THERE IS NOT EVIDENCE TO SUPPORT THIS CONTENTION. Proposed order submitted by moving party. If plaintiff does not agree with the form of order, plaintiff may submit its own for the Court to determine. (In addition, the Court indicated that with minor exceptions, the objections to evidence were overruled.)
(302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-23-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT DAVID SHERMAN DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Appearances: Sheila M. Bari, Esq. of Bradshaw & Associates (415) 433-4800, appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; Paul Melbostad, Esq. & A. Jeanne Jorge-Grove, Esq. of Goldstein, Gellamn, Melbostad, Gibson & Harris, LLP (415) 673-5600, appearing for Defendant and moving party; Linda B. Oliver, of ReedSmith LLP (415) 659-4819, appearing for Wells Fargo and Company, & Minnesota Life Ins. Co. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer is sustained with 10 days leave to amend as to 3rd cause of action for fraud and 4th cause of action for constructive fraud. The alleged misrepresentation on which they are based do not state a claim with respect to plaintiff. Plaintiff does not allege that a misrepresentation was made to him or that he relied on it. Demurrer overruled as to all other causes of action. Order signed in open Court.(302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-28-2010 Continued To Jul-23-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 212

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-09-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT DAVID SHERMAN DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Appearances: Sheila Bari, Esq. of Bradshaw & Associates (415) 433-4800, appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; A. Jeanne Jorge, Esq. of Goldstein, Gellamn, Melbostad, Gibson & Harris, LLP (415) 673-5600, appearing for Defendant and Moving party; Linda B. Oliver, Esq. of ReedSmith LLP (415) 659-4819, appearing for Wells Fargo. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer overruled regarding standing issue. Executrix's final account and report describes her actions in item 7 assigning the rights of the estate to plaintiff. Judge approved of executrix's actions in order dated September 29, 2009. Demurrer sustained with ten days leave to amend as to 2nd, 3rd, 4th, 5th, 7th and 8th causes of action. Insufficient facts pled that defendant intentionally disrupted or breached a relationship between plaintiff and Wells Fargo, or that a relationship existed. Fraud must be supported by specific facts, and constructive fraud must be shown by misleading conduct or an inherently fraudulent act or omission. Facts pled do not show defendant's retention of the insurance proceeds was unjust. Plaintiff does not allege facts sufficient to show he had possession of the insurance proceeds or that the disposition was wrongful. Plaintiff must allege wrongful acts defendant performed to obtain the loan and receive the insurance proceeds. Order signed in open Court.(302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797
